images.title: Otherworldly Folds
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, OM 100-400 f5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: Looking this closely to a jelly fungi makes you wonder if they are really alien in nature. Whether they are from this world or another, the textures and folds can mesmerize endlessly.

images.title: Twists
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 40-150 f2.8
images.field_notes: The soft twists on the Ohio River bring up a calm and relaxing feeling that can only be found by the water.

images.title: Shells
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 12-40 f2.8
images.field_notes: A collection of small shells on the coast of Washington state. The abundance of them in this small patch felt like a gift.

images.title: Textured Shore
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: Taken in Seattle, looking down to the shore, the textures took over.

images.title: Green
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, OM 100-400 f5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: The soft algae while small, can feel so much bigger. When examined closer it can remind you of an entire forest when viewed from the air.

images.title: Lines in the Wood
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 75 f1.8
images.field_notes: The wet side of the log made for a sharp contrast to the dry. One side loses itself to the darkeness, the other reveals it's soft wood carved by the water in the light.

images.title: Stone
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 75 f1.8
images.field_notes: You can lose yourself in any stone if you look close enough, but the color variation on this one is what brought the lens closer.

images.title: Minneapolis in Frame
images.gear: OM-3, Olympus 75 f1.8
images.field_notes: A frame seemed to showcase the skyscrapers in the background perfectly. This photo captures a real piece of art, which lives as you walk by. Which buildings are in the frame depends on where you are as you pass.

images.title: Latticed Locks
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 40-150 f2.8 + MC-14
images.field_notes: What can't be seen is the power that these locks hold back.

images.title: Louisville Lights
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 40-150 f2.8 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Taken during a blood moon when the eclipse was hiding. The slow shutter lets the lights of the cars draw out.

images.title: Walking Bridge
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 40-150 f2.8 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Before the runners show up in the morning the walking bridge is a quiet place, waiting for the day to begin.

images.title: Navidad Beach Houses
images.field_notes: A whole day walking the beach. When heading home a quick glance to where we came from gave this photo.

images.title: 2nd Street Blood Moon
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 40-150 f2.8 + MC-14
images.field_notes: There were hours of planning, and a day of scouting to find the right location for the eclipse. This is not it. The moon was hiding for most of the eclipse, and by the time it decided to show the original shot was no longer possible. Making the best of the moment brought this shot.

images.title: Form
images.gear: OM-3, Olympus 75 f1.8
images.field_notes: A structure jutts out into the sky. The stark contrast between the sky and the building feels sharp and unprovoked. The building practically begging to be noticed.

images.title: Metal and Glass
images.gear: OM-3, Olympus 75 f1.8
images.field_notes: When inspecting this building the sharp but broken eyes engaged my eyes.

images.title: To Medano
images.gear: OM-3, OM 17 f1.8 II
images.location: Great Sand Dune National Park, CO
images.field_notes: Taken on the dunes, looking out towards a distant mountain lake. You can't see it as it's behind the mountains, but the wish for an alpine dip is still there.

images.title: Portland Head House
images.gear: OM-1, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: A stormy day at the most photographed lighthouse in the world. The winds were blowing strong, and the ocean was crashing, yet the lighthouse still shines.

images.title: Atacama Layers
images.field_notes: Taken on the salt flats looking to Bolivia, the flamingos in the foreground add to the scale.

images.title: Chilean Coast
images.gear: OM-1, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: A cliff and the ocean always seem to make sense. Looking down on the beach far below, and seeing the waves roll in, I can still practically taste the salty air when I look at this photo.

images.title: Washington Fog
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 12-40 f2.8
images.field_notes: After spending a few days on the beach, the fog moved in signaling it was time to leave. The night came early that day with the hike out under the cover of darkness.

images.title: Garden Bridge
images.gear: OM-3, Olympus 75 f1.8
images.field_notes: While in the Japanese Garden this bridge seemed to beckon for a photo.

images.title: Waves over Rocks
images.field_notes: On the cliffs above looking down to where the water broke against the rocky shore. The slow shutter speed lets the water soften over the sharp rocks.

images.title: Washington Light Beams
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: Capturing not only these peaceful beams of light, but the memory of a morning walk on the coast.

images.title: Curtain Falls
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: A bit of a detour, but one that I'm glad to have taken. Earlier this trip I came across a family of three brothers and a father, who take an annual trip to BWCA for the last few decades. They expressed regret not going further to see Curtain Falls. This regret prompted me to change course, and this photograph is for them.

images.title: End of the Cape
images.field_notes: The last bit of Cape Cod. The stillness can be felt through this photo.

images.title: Valle de la Luna
images.gear: OM-1, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: Valley of the Moon. The name speaks for itself. We were ushered out shortly as the weather quickly changed to thunderstorms.

images.title: Dunes, Study of Sand
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5
images.location: Great Sand Dune National Park, CO
images.field_notes: The dark, the light, the sand, the wind, the solo hiker.

images.title: Fern
images.gear: OM-3, Olympus 75 f1.8
images.field_notes: The fern itself felt still and quiet, yet still requested attention.

images.title: Bulbous
images.field_notes: Distracted at first by a spider, then by the puff mushroom he walked by gave me this. The dark background give sharp contrast to the puffy mushroom.

images.title: Flower, Soft
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 12-40 f2.8
images.field_notes: Taken on the coast of Washington, this was the only flower seen the whole trip.

images.title: Irish Goodbye
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 40-150 f2.8 + MC-20
images.field_notes: While at the National Arboretum this butterfly quietly exited the scene as so many of us do.

images.title: Swamp Flower
images.field_notes: Swamps can be places of beauty if you look closely enough, or just at all really.

images.title: Approaching
images.location: Great Sand Dune National Park, CO
images.field_notes: This tree standing by itself outside of the dunes. The tall grass distracted at first, placing it more prominently made it feel like you were spying on the tree.

images.title: Starfish
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 12-40 f2.8
images.field_notes: Who doesn't love a starfish?

images.title: Swamp Flower pt. 2
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 40-150 f2.8 + MC-20
images.field_notes: It doesn't take much to see how a swamp can be beautiful.

images.title: Present
images.gear: OM-1, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: Above the trails this tree sits, greeting guests who braved the iron rungs.

images.title: BWCA Sky pt. 1
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: The sky in the Boundary Waters is always a pleasure to behold.

images.title: BWCA Sky pt. 2
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 17 f1.8
images.field_notes: The small shooting stars or satellites can be seen in the lower left.

images.title: Blood Moon
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Taken during a blood moon, it was hiding behind a cloud before coming out unexpectedly.

images.title: BWCA Sky pt. 3
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 8mm f1.8
images.field_notes: The fisheye view can be extremely useful for these extra wide angle shots of the night sky.

images.title: Washington Coast Stars
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 8mm f1.8
images.field_notes: The Milky Way on the most northwestern point in the continental US.

images.title: BWCA Sky pt. 4
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, Olympus 8mm f1.8
images.location: Great Sand Dunes National Park, CO
images.field_notes: More fisheye of the BWCA night sky.

images.title: Dunes Moon
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400
images.location: Great Sand Dunes National Park, CO
images.field_notes: A daytime shot of the moon coming out in the Great Sand Dunes.

images.title: Falmingo in Flight, Layered
images.field_notes: As flamingos flew all around us, this one happened to line up perfectly with the layers in the mountains behind.

images.title: Window
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM150-400 f4.5
images.location: The Parklands of Floyds Fork, KY
images.field_notes: Taken by sheer luck, this Belted Kingfisher passed through the hole in the trees for a brief moment.

images.title: Western Bluebird
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5
images.location: Great Sand Dunes National Park, CO
images.field_notes: The electric blue plumage of the Western Bluebird is always a pleasure to witness.

images.title: Friend
images.field_notes: As the kayakers moved through the bay, this seal peaked up to check on them.

images.title: Great Egret Departure
images.gear: E-M1 Mark III, OM 100-400 f5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: As the Egret leaves, it feels like it is falling. Although it does briefly descend, this is all done for it to take to the sky.

images.title: Lizard
images.field_notes: Seen on the salt flats, the texture of the lizard's skin begged for a photo.

images.title: Comorant Lovers
images.field_notes: Looking out to the Pacific, this rock was so far away it could barely be discerned what was on it. After going through them seeing the two cuddled up was perfect.

images.title: Dinner, Alone
images.field_notes: Seeing Ospreys always excites, they are probably my favorite raptor. This one had found it's dinner and was taking it somewhere private to eat.

images.title: Oystercatcher
images.field_notes: The Oystercatchers were out this day all around us, this particular one played by the crashing waves.

images.title: The Greatest Egret
images.field_notes: A beautiful capture of the aigrettes, the fine ornamental feathers, with the light highlighting them perfectly.

images.title: Short-eared Owl
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5
images.field_notes: Watching owls is always a treat, this one was special as it came down to a post nearby, almost as if it was posing.

images.title: Ibis
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5
images.field_notes: When a vagrant comes to town you visit. This one approched withing inches after lying in the mud waiting for the right shot.

images.title: Herb
images.field_notes: Herb. North Atlantic Right Whale #1250. A 40 year veteran of the Bay of Fundy, seeing one of these rare creatures in the wild will alter your perception.

images.title: Swallows
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching the swallows play by the pond made by beavers one was spotted still on the branches in the middle. After focusing on it the mother came by to feed her young in the perfect right place, right time scenario.

images.title: Sandhill Crane
images.field_notes: A dinosaur of a bird. Listening to thousands of them all at the smae time is an incredible noise you cannot forget.

images.title: Nesting
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5
images.field_notes: The dedication of these parents to raise their young is always a sight to behold. Although she is staying with the nest, dad is always nearby, and ready to bring dinner.

images.title: Baltimore Oriole
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5
images.field_notes: The bright colors of the Baltimore Oriole is always such a delight. The perfect contrast from the orange of the feathers to the blue sky is perfect.

images.title: Screech Owl
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5 + MC-14
images.location: The Parklands of Floyds Fork, KY
images.field_notes: This particular fellow stays guard over the bridge. Everyday hundreds of people pass by, none the wiser to his presence.

images.title: Shared Waters
images.gear: OM-1 Mark II, OM 150-400 f4.5 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching the Ospreys hunt this day was a treat, and seeing them grab a meal right behind their human counterpart was a bonus.

images.title: Borb pt. 1
images.field_notes: On this particularly cold day the birds came out after a long night of snow. This White-throated Sparrow shows how it stays warm by poofing up.

images.title: Bolted Braces
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.40-150mm F2.8 + MC-14

images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.40-150mm F2.8 + MC-14

images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: The electric blue plumage of the Western Bluebird is always striking to see, especially in contrast to the neutrals of the dunes.

images.title: Baltimore Oriole
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: The bright colors of the Baltimore Oriole is always such a delight. The perfect contrast from the orange of the feathers to the blue sky is perfect.

images.title: Swallow Swallowing a Swallow
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching the swallows play by the pond, one was spotted still on the branches in the middle. After focusing in on it, the mother came by to feed her young in the right place, right time scenario.

images.title: Shared Waters
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching Ospreys hunt is always incredible. Seeing them show up their human counterparts right in front of you is a bonus.

images.title: Ibis

images.title: Screech Owl
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC + MC-14

images.field_notes: This particular fellow stands guard over the bridge. Everyday hundreds of people pass by, none the wiser to his presence.
